docs.title: Home
docs.text:
DistrictSync

Convert MyEducation BC General Data Extracts to SpacesEDU Advanced CSV — automatically, every night.

[Download for Windows](https://github.com/myblueprint-spaces/DistrictSync/releases/latest/download/DistrictSync-windows.exe){ .md-button } [View on GitHub](https://github.com/myblueprint-spaces/DistrictSync){ .md-button .md-button--secondary } 7CSV outputs 6District configs 3Platforms 91%Test coverage

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
Get the latest release for your platform:

Platform Download Notes Windows DistrictSync-windows.exe Double-click to open Setup Wizard Linux DistrictSync-linux chmod +x before first run macOS DistrictSync-macos Allow in System Settings › Privacy & Security

First time?

Start with the Partner Installation Guide for step-by-step setup instructions including the Setup Wizard walkthrough.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
GDE files  →  DistrictSync  →  SpacesEDU CSV files  →  SFTP upload
Input (MyEdBC GDE) Output (SpacesEDU) Tier Student Demographic Students.csv rostering Staff Information – Enhanced Staff.csv rostering Emergency Contact Information Family.csv rostering Student Schedule + Course Information Classes.csv rostering Student Schedule + Class Information – Enhanced Enrollments.csv rostering Course Information CourseInfo.csv myBlueprint+ Course History + Selection + Information StudentCourses.csv myBlueprint+

docs.title: Output tiers
docs.text:
Which CSVs DistrictSync produces is controlled by enabled_entities in the config:

Config Outputs myedbc (and inheriting district configs sd40myedbc, sd48myedbc, …) The 5 rostering CSVs mbp_all 5 rostering CSVs + CourseInfo.csv + StudentCourses.csv (full myBlueprint+ tier) mbp_core Students.csv + CourseInfo.csv + StudentCourses.csv only (minimal myBlueprint+ tier)

A district with non-standard file naming AND the myBlueprint+ tier composes the two — create a child config with _base: pointing to the district config and override enabled_entities.

docs.title: Key features
docs.text:
• Active student filtering — Active and PreReg students exported; Inactive and other statuses excluded
  • CEDS grade mapping — BC grade codes (K, 1–12) mapped to CEDS standard format
  • Blended class detection — same teacher/time/multi-grade sections merged automatically
  • Homeroom generation — elementary homeroom classes auto-generated from demographics
  • District config inheritance — district-specific overrides layer on top of the base MyEdBC config
  • Automated scheduling — runs daily via Windows Task Scheduler or cron
  • SFTP upload — zips all CSVs into a single dated file and uploads to SpacesEDU after each run; host allowlist restricts uploads to SpacesEDU servers only
  • Atomic writes — all-or-nothing commit; a failed run never leaves partial output
  • Anomaly detection — warns when output record counts drop more than 20% compared to the previous run
  • Structured run logging — each run writes a machine-readable JSON log tag for the Run History page
  • Mapping Editor — web UI wizard for creating or modifying district configs without editing YAML
  • Help & Docs page — in-app access to documentation and support links

docs.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD
docs.text:
Releases are fully automated. As a developer you simply:

git tag v1.x.0
git push origin --tags

GitHub Actions then:

  1. Runs the full test suite (Python 3.9, 3.11, 3.13) + lint + format check + type check + security scan (bandit) + config validation
  2. Builds platform executables — Windows .exe, Linux binary, macOS binary — in parallel
  3. Creates a GitHub Release with all three files attached and download links in the release notes

Partners can always get the latest version from the Releases page — the /releases/latest/download/ URL always resolves to the most recent release.

docs.text: 📦 Partner Guide Installation · Troubleshooting · FAQ 🛠 Developer Guide Setup · Architecture · Releases 🏫 SpacesEDU spacesEDU.com · support@myBlueprint.ca

docs.title: Architecture Tree
docs.text:
Single-source index of the codebase: every source file with a one-line description. Read this first to locate relevant files instead of exploring blindly. Keep it current — adding/moving/removing a file requires updating this index in the same change (enforced in CI via make check-tree).

Last generated from main @ c669404.

docs.title: Entry point
docs.text: • src/main.py — CLI entry point: parses argparse flags and dispatches to run_pipeline or SFTP subcommands (--sftp-configure, --sftp-test, --sftp-show); exits code 3 when SFTP delivery fails (PipelineResult.sftp_attempted and not sftp_ok); re-exports pipeline symbols for backward compatibility; PyInstaller __main__ target.

docs.title: src/etl/  (ETL pipeline)
docs.text:
• src/etl/extractor.py — DataExtractor: parses each GDE file (CSV/TXT) through one bytes-based core with multi-encoding fallback (UTF-8 → Latin1 → CP1252), auto-delimiter detection, and unquoted-Section malformed-row repair; two public entrypoints share that core — load_data (disk) and load_from_bytes (in-memory, e.g. browser uploads); normalises column names immediately after load; raises ExtractionError on unparse-able content.
  • src/etl/transformer.py — DataTransformer facade: backward-compatible wrapper that delegates to entity-specific transformers via TransformContext and BlendedClassDetector; exposes school-year / homeroom state properties for direct test access.
  • src/etl/pipeline.py — Core ETL orchestration. run_pipeline runs Extractor → Transformer → Loader + anomaly/diff/quality + JSON run-log + optional SFTP → PipelineResult (exit 1 = no usable input, exit 3 = SFTP failure). run_transform(...) -> TransformOutputs (outputs/field_orders/data_errors) is the shared transform seam; compute_anomalies() single-sources the row-drop check (CLI + Convert).
  • src/etl/loader.py — DataLoader: writes field-ordered CSVs (utf-8-sig BOM; plain UTF-8 for _NO_BOM_ENTITIES=StudentAttendance). csv_encoding() + select_ordered() single-source the BOM rule + fail-loud column selection. save_all() stages to .tmp_<ts>/, then _commit_staged commits with backup-and-restore atomicity (.bak_<ts>/ + os.replace, rollback on failure); archive_stale_outputs() archives non-current CSVs.
  • src/etl/column_names.py — Canonical string constants for GDE source-column names (e.g. MASTER_TIMETABLE_ID, STAFF_SOURCEID, COURSE_CODE) to avoid magic strings across transformers.

docs.title: src/etl/transformers/
docs.text:
• src/etl/transformers/base.py — BaseTransformer ABC with shared utilities: CEDS grade mapping, ALLOWED_TRANSFORMS allowlist, apply_field_map() (row-resilient + fail-loud: a bad row blanks only its cell, errors → context.data_errors, never silent), assign_class_ids(), excluded-course filters, the active-student predicate, and filter_to_active() (zero-orphan roster filter for Classes + Enrollments).
  • src/etl/transformers/context.py — TransformContext dataclass: mutable shared state for one pipeline run (school year, academic dates, raw data frames, active_student_ids roster published by Students, homeroom/blended maps, and data_errors — the per-run fail-loud field-transform ledger surfaced by the pipeline) passed between entity transformers.
  • src/etl/transformers/registry.py — TRANSFORMER_REGISTRY dict and get_transformer(): maps entity names to singleton transformer instances; falls back to DefaultTransformer (field-map-only) for any unregistered entity.
  • src/etl/transformers/students.py — StudentTransformer: filters to active students (Active + PreReg by default; active_values configurable) via the shared BaseTransformer predicate (status wins; withdraw-date is a fallback); publishes the active roster to context.active_student_ids (zero-orphan); generates emails; normalises Date of Birth to ISO.
  • src/etl/transformers/staff.py — StaffTransformer: optionally merges staff info with a roster file to resolve staff sourceid, then applies field map with map_role transform (Y → teacher, else → administrator).
  • src/etl/transformers/family.py — FamilyTransformer: thin field-map-only transformer for parent/guardian emergency-contact GDE rows.
  • src/etl/transformers/classes.py — ClassTransformer: orchestrates blended detection, homeroom class generation (configured grades, filtered to context.active_student_ids so no all-inactive homeroom class is created), subject class generation (schedule + course + staff join), and emits blended classes from context; deduplicates on Class ID.
  • src/etl/transformers/enrollments.py — EnrollmentTransformer: builds homeroom enrollments (from student demographic) and subject enrollments (from schedule) — both with student rows filtered to context.active_student_ids (zero-orphan invariant; teacher rows derive from the unfiltered frames) — plus co-teacher/blended teacher enrollments (from class info context); deduplicates on (Class ID, User ID, Role).
  • src/etl/transformers/blended.py — BlendedClassDetector: identifies same-teacher/same-time-slot sections with 2+ grade levels; populates context.blended_class_map, blended_class_metadata, and blended_teacher_map; falls back to deduplicated schedule when ClassInfo is absent.
  • src/etl/transformers/course_info.py — CourseInfoTransformer: filters course rows by excluded_course_code_patterns regex list, applies field map, deduplicates on (Course Code, School ID); produces the myBlueprint+ CourseInfo CSV.
  • src/etl/transformers/student_courses.py — StudentCoursesTransformer: ports SD62 PowerShell history/selection join logic; two-pass (history then selection), section-stripping + flavor-truncation course-code cleaning, two-tier CourseInfo lookup; produces the myBlueprint+ StudentCourses CSV.
  • src/etl/transformers/student_attendance.py — StudentAttendanceTransformer: opt-in SpacesEDU feed unioning two optional bands resolved BY source_files role (daily_absences/period_absences), order-independent (daily/period/both), each band's config required only when present — K-7 Daily derives category+rows (fail-loud on unmapped code), 8-12 Period passes through per-period; 4-col StudentAttendance.csv (required only), no dedup.

docs.title: src/config/
docs.text:
• src/config/models.py — Pydantic v2 models for YAML mapping validation: MappingConfig, GlobalConfig, EntityConfig, and the discriminated field-mapping variants (transform, fixed value, academic year, append year, email format, name config, id-role pair, enroll-status [strict, extra="forbid"], plus bare str/null); classify_field() dispatcher; to_raw_dict()/get_raw_field_map() for pipeline consumption.
  • src/config/loader.py — load_config(sis_type): discovers YAML from user-overrides dir then bundled dir; resolves _base inheritance via recursive deep-merge with cycle detection; validates via Pydantic; exposes available_configs() for the UI district picker.
  • src/config/app_config.py — AppConfig dataclass: persists non-sensitive runtime settings (paths, SIS type, schedule, SFTP host/port/user) to ~/.districtsync/config.json with OS-safe permissions; SFTP password is never stored here (keyring only).

docs.title: src/quality/
docs.text: • src/quality/report.py — DataQualityReport / EntityReport: checks missing/empty fields (warns at >50% threshold), entity-specific duplicate detection, orphaned enrollments (class or user not found in outputs), and grade distribution anomalies.

docs.title: src/sftp/
docs.text: • src/sftp/uploader.py — SFTPUploader: paramiko SSHClient to an ALLOWED_SFTP_HOSTS host; passwords via OS keyring (KEYRING_SERVICE); zips the rostering CSVs into districtsync_<sis>_YYYY-MM-DD.zip and uploads any StudentAttendance.csv standalone outside the zip to the same remote dir (SpacesEDU checks it by name); exposes test_connection(), upload_csvs(), get_stored_password().

docs.title: src/scheduler/
docs.text:
• src/scheduler/windows.py — Windows Task Scheduler: register_task runs a FIXED PowerShell Register-ScheduledTask script (_build_register_script() via -EncodedCommand, reads only $env:DSYNC_* child env); password → Password/Highest, no-password → Interactive/Limited (never S4U); errors de-CLIXML'd (catch→[Console]::Error.WriteLine + _clean_ps_stderr()); is_elevated(); delete_task/query_task on schtasks.exe.
  • src/scheduler/linux.py — Linux/macOS cron integration: register_cron() / delete_cron() append/remove a sentinel-tagged crontab entry via the system crontab command; uses shlex.quote() for safe shell escaping.

docs.title: src/utils/
docs.text:
• src/utils/validators.py — Centralised security validators: ALLOWED_SFTP_HOSTS allowlist, validate_sis_type(), validate_task_name(), validate_run_time(), validate_sftp_host(), quote_for_shell(); all user-supplied values flowing into subprocess or SFTP must pass through here.
  • src/utils/logger.py — get_logger(): configures logging from config/logging.conf (or falls back to basicConfig) writing to the canonical absolute path ~/.districtsync/etl_tool.log so logs persist across PyInstaller restarts and scheduled-task runs.
  • src/utils/helpers.py — General-purpose utilities: normalize_columns(), ensure_directory(), validate_csv(), validate_path(), safe_float_conversion(), district_slug(), build_zip_name().
  • src/utils/paths.py — Single source of truth for path resolution: bundle_root(), bundle_config_dir(), bundle_mappings_dir(), user_data_dir(), user_mappings_dir(), user_log_file(); works identically in source-install and frozen-exe (PyInstaller _MEIPASS) environments.

docs.title: src/ui/
docs.text:
• src/ui/Home.py — Streamlit multi-page app entry point (streamlit run src/ui/Home.py): renders status dashboard (configured/unconfigured banner, last Windows scheduled-task run, SFTP status); auto-discovers pages/ subdirectory; starts the idle-shutdown watchdog once per server process (@st.cache_resource) and renders the sidebar Exit control.
  • src/ui/brand.py — myBlueprint/SpacesEDU brand styles: inject_brand_css() injects corporate colour palette and card styles; header() renders the branded page heading with wordmark; sidebar_exit_control() renders the shared Exit DistrictSync sidebar button (→ lifecycle.request_exit); step_progress() renders a numbered step bar.
  • src/ui/folder_picker.py — pick_directory(): native OS folder-selection dialog (tkinter askdirectory, lazily imported) for the local Setup Wizard's path inputs; returns None on cancel/no-GUI so callers fall back to manual text entry.
  • src/ui/launcher.py — PyInstaller UI launcher: locates src/ui/Home.py inside the frozen bundle and invokes Streamlit programmatically with --server.headless=false; used when the binary is launched without CLI arguments. Single-instance guard: if lifecycle.already_running(8501) (health body == ok) it opens a browser to the existing instance and exits (no second server).
  • src/ui/lifecycle.py — Graceful-UI-shutdown lifecycle: pure should_exit(); start_idle_watchdog() reaps the server after last-browser disconnect (logged no-op on Streamlit-internal change); write_guard()/safe_to_exit() block exit during atomic save_all; request_exit(); already_running() health-body single-instance guard. Isolates the PRIVATE Runtime._session_mgr.num_active_sessions() reach to one helper (streamlit <1.55).
  • src/ui/mapping_helpers.py — Mapping Editor support library: detect_columns() (headerless heuristic), get_field_metadata() (field descriptions/types), build_override_dict() (diff vs base config), save_mapping_yaml(), column_selectbox() widget, SOURCE_FILE_ROLES and CEDS_GRADES constants.

docs.title: src/ui/pages/
docs.text:
• src/ui/pages/01_Setup_Wizard.py — 5-step setup wizard: file paths → district config → schedule time (Windows collects account password + run-as user for register_task; blank warns logged-on-only) → SFTP config (Step 4 verifies credential) → summary/activation; dashboard. _classify_schedule_error(msg, elevated) maps a failure to an elevation-aware message. On schedule-success shows a Finish & Close button (→ lifecycle.request_exit).
  • src/ui/pages/02_Convert.py — Ad-hoc conversion page: a thin adapter over the shared ETL engine (uploaded GDE bytes → load_from_bytes → run_transform → DataLoader, so download/zip + SFTP use csv_encoding and match the CLI byte-for-byte); renders quality report + diff, offers ZIP download / optional SFTP upload.
  • src/ui/pages/03_Run_History.py — Run History page: parses __DISTRICTSYNC_RUN__ JSON log lines from ~/.districtsync/etl_tool.log into a table (raw-tail fallback). The display-only Status cell shows amber "ETL OK · SFTP FAILED" on delivery failure and "Completed with N data errors" on field-transform errors, so the headline never contradicts the exit code.
  • src/ui/pages/04_Mapping_Editor.py — 7-step visual Mapping Editor: guides non-technical users through entity selection, file upload + column detection, field mapping, academic calendar, and name/email config; saves a minimal _base-inheriting override YAML to ~/.districtsync/mappings/.
  • src/ui/pages/05_Help.py — Help page: renders docs/ markdown files (partner guides + developer docs) in the Streamlit UI; single source of truth shared with the MkDocs static site.

docs.title: config/mappings/
docs.text:
• config/mappings/myedbc_mapping.yaml — Base config (v1.9): defines all 7 entity templates (Students, Staff, Family, Classes, Enrollments, CourseInfo, StudentCourses); enabled_entities defaults to the 5 standard rostering CSVs; sets homeroom grades, school-year source, and course-code exclusion patterns.
  • config/mappings/sd40myedbc_mapping.yaml — SD40 New Westminster override (_base: myedbc): CSV source file names, headerless schedule with injected column headers, {student number}@newwestschools.ca email, excluded_course_codes for ATT--AM/PM/Daily attendance rows.
  • config/mappings/sd48myedbc_mapping.yaml — SD48 Sea to Sky override (_base: myedbc): remaps to StudentDemographicEnhanced.txt and StaffInformation.txt; no other deviations from base.
  • config/mappings/sd51myedbc_mapping.yaml — SD51 Boundary override (_base: myedbc): StudentDemographicEnhanced.txt, {student number}@sd51.bc.ca email, fixed hardcoded academic start/end dates (bypasses auto-detection).
  • config/mappings/sd54myedbc_mapping.yaml — SD54 Bulkley Valley override (_base: myedbc): lowercase source file names (studentschedule, courseinformation, staffinformation, classinformationenhanced .txt), non-Enhanced staffinformation for Staff, EmergencyContactInformationEnhanced for Family, {legal surname}.{usual first name}@sd54.bc.ca email, ATT--AM/PM/Daily excluded; academic dates auto-derive from School Year.
  • config/mappings/sd74myedbc_mapping.yaml — SD74 Gold Trail override (_base: myedbc): swapped legal/usual name fields, {student number}@sd74.bc.ca email, studentcourseselection.txt as schedule source, ClassInfoEnhanced.txt, ParentInformation.txt, fixed academic dates.
  • config/mappings/mbp_all_mapping.yaml — myBlueprint+ full tier (_base: myedbc): extends enabled_entities to all 7 (adds CourseInfo + StudentCourses on top of the standard 5 rostering CSVs).
  • config/mappings/mbp_core_mapping.yaml — myBlueprint+ minimal tier (_base: myedbc): enabled_entities = [Students, CourseInfo, StudentCourses] only; for districts that need course history/selection but not full class rosters.
  • config/mappings/mbponly_mapping.yaml — myBlueprint+ courses-only tier (_base: myedbc): enabled_entities = [CourseInfo, StudentCourses] only (no Students); requires only CourseInformation.txt + StudentCourseHistory.txt + StudentCourseSelection.txt.
  • config/mappings/sd51attendance_mapping.yaml — SD51 attendance-only tier (_base: sd51myedbc): enabled_entities = [StudentAttendance] only; generates just StudentAttendance.csv from the two absence GDEs, independent of the rostering pipeline (no rostering GDEs needed).

docs.title: Root
docs.text:
• pyproject.toml — Project metadata (name=districtsync, version=3.2.0), setuptools build config, pytest settings (addopts, benchmarks deselected, coverage omits), ruff lint/format rules, mypy config, bandit exclusions.
  • Makefile — Developer shortcuts: install, test, test-cov, lint, fmt, ui, build-win, clean, validate-config, docs, docs-serve.
  • requirements.txt — Runtime dependencies: pandas, PyYAML, python-dateutil, pydantic, paramiko, keyring, streamlit.
  • requirements-dev.txt — Dev/CI dependencies: extends requirements.txt with pytest, pytest-cov, ruff, mypy, bandit, types-paramiko, types-PyYAML, hypothesis, pytest-benchmark, and optional UI-test extras (playwright, pytest-sftpserver).
  • mkdocs.yml — MkDocs configuration: site name, GitHub repo URL, navigation structure (partner guides + developer docs), Material theme, auto-deploy to GitHub Pages on release tag.
  • README.md — Project overview, quick-start instructions, supported districts, and links to full documentation.
  • CHANGELOG.md — Keep-a-Changelog release history; per-release behavior changes (GitHub Releases holds download links + auto-generated commit notes).

docs.title: tests/
docs.text:
• tests/conftest.py — Shared fixtures (synthetic DataFrames, YAML configs, DataTransformer instances) for all tests; also hosts the streamlit_server session fixture for UI smoke tests.
  • tests/snapshots/generate_synthetic.py — Script to regenerate synthetic SD74 GDE input files in tests/snapshots/input/ (run once after schema changes).
  • tests/snapshots/ — Frozen SD74 snapshot data: input/ holds 6 synthetic GDE files (StudentDemographic, Staff, Family, Classes, Schedule, CourseInfo); output/ holds 5 golden CSV files (Students, Staff, Family, Classes, Enrollments) locked against regression.
  • tests/snapshots/mbp_input/ — Small hand-authored synthetic GDEs for the mbponly course tier (CourseInformation, StudentCourseHistory, StudentCourseSelection); consumed by the mbponly end-to-end pipeline test.
  • tests/test_config.py — Config model and loader: Pydantic validation of YAML structure, classify_field() dispatch, _base inheritance deep-merge, cycle detection.
  • tests/test_config_loader_multi_dir.py — Two-tier config discovery: user-dir override wins over bundled, _base resolution across search dirs, available_configs() deduplication.
  • tests/test_pipeline_e2e.py — Full ETL e2e with synthetic on-disk GDE files: verifies output CSV structure and data for the standard myedbc config.
  • tests/test_pipeline_e2e_districts.py — District-specific e2e: verifies sd48 and sd74 district configs produce all 5 expected CSVs from synthetic GDE files using district-specific filenames.
  • tests/test_pipeline_e2e_mbponly.py — mbponly tier e2e smoke test: runs the pipeline against tests/snapshots/mbp_input/ and asserts it emits only CourseInfo.csv + StudentCourses.csv (no rostering CSVs) with the right schema and required-files set.
  • tests/test_regression_sd74.py — SD74 golden-file regression: runs the pipeline against tests/snapshots/input/ and diffs against tests/snapshots/output/ (schema + values).
  • tests/test_contract.py — Output schema contract: asserts every district config produces exactly the required SpacesEDU Advanced CSV column set — no missing columns, no unexpected extras.
  • tests/test_transform_students.py — Students transformer: enrollment-status filtering, active-student logic, email generation, Date of Birth normalisation.
  • tests/test_transform_staff.py — Staff transformer: field mapping, roster merge for staff sourceid, role mapping.
  • tests/test_transform_family.py — Family transformer: field mapping from emergency-contact GDE rows.
  • tests/test_transform_classes.py — Classes transformer: homeroom generation, subject class creation, blended class integration.
  • tests/test_transform_enrollments.py — Enrollments transformer: homeroom/subject/co-teacher enrollments, deduplication on (Class ID, User ID, Role).
  • tests/test_transform_course_info.py — CourseInfo transformer: course-code pattern exclusion, deduplication on (Course Code, School ID); uses synthetic MyEd BC CourseInformation data.
  • tests/test_transform_student_courses.py — StudentCourses transformer: history/selection join logic, W-mark skipping, section-stripping, flavor truncation, CourseInfo two-tier lookup.
  • tests/test_transform_base.py — BaseTransformer shared utilities: filter_excluded_course_code_patterns() and clean_course_code_flavor() (other helpers are covered indirectly by entity tests).
  • tests/test_blended_classes.py — Blended class detection: detection correctness, naming convention, grade-range merging, validation.
  • tests/test_class_generation.py — Class ID and class name generation, 100-char name truncation.
  • tests/test_grade_mapping.py — CEDS grade-code mapping (grade_to_ceds), edge cases and unknown grades.
  • tests/test_email_generation.py — Student email template rendering (format field type), various template patterns.
  • tests/test_enrollment_status.py — Enrollment status determination: Active/PreReg/Inactive via enrolment status column and 4-format withdrawal-date fallback.
  • tests/test_zero_orphan_enrollments.py — Zero-orphan invariant: Students publishes the active roster and Classes (homeroom) + Enrollments (homeroom + subject) filter their student rows against it; asserts no student enrollment references a non-rostered User ID, teacher/co-teacher rows stay byte-identical, all-inactive homerooms produce no class, and the empty-roster guard leaves rows intact + warns.
  • tests/test_role_mapping.py — Staff role mapping (Y → teacher, else → administrator) and User ID / User Role pair generation.
  • tests/test_school_year.py — School year determination from schedule data and calendar-date heuristic; academic start/end date calculation; datetime mock patterns.
  • tests/test_extractor.py — DataExtractor multi-encoding/delimiter fallback, headerless file injection, ExtractionError on unparse-able files.
  • tests/test_loader.py — DataLoader CSV output, field ordering, atomic save_all() commit/rollback behaviour.
  • tests/test_quality_report.py — DataQualityReport checks: missing fields, duplicates, orphaned enrollments, grade distribution.
  • tests/test_validators.py — All validators in src/utils/validators.py: SIS type, task name, run time, SFTP host allowlist, shell quoting.
  • tests/test_app_config.py — AppConfig load/save round-trip, unknown-field tolerance, default values.
  • tests/test_main_helpers.py — Pipeline helper functions: _check_anomalies, _emit_run_log, extract_required_files, _sftp_upload, _print_diff.
  • tests/test_cli.py — CLI flags: --dry-run, --diff, --quality, --version (calls run_pipeline() directly, bypasses argparse).
  • tests/test_sftp_uploader.py — SFTPUploader with mocked paramiko and keyring: store/retrieve password, test_connection(), upload_csvs() zip-and-put flow.
  • tests/test_sftp_cli.py — SFTP CLI subcommands: --sftp-configure (env var + stdin password sources), --sftp-test, --sftp-show, host allowlist rejection, flag mutual-exclusion.
  • tests/test_sftp_integration.py — Live SFTP integration using pytest-sftpserver (real paramiko transport); skipped automatically if the package is absent.
  • tests/test_schedulers.py — Windows Task Scheduler and Linux cron wrappers with all subprocess calls mocked.
  • tests/test_scheduler_runas.py — Unit tests for register_task run-as behavior: asserts /RU /RP /RL HIGHEST flags when a password is supplied, omitted when not (back-compat), password never appears in captured logs (only ***), and validate_run_as_user accepts/rejects correctly.
  • tests/test_sftp_exit.py — CLI exit-code tests for SFTP failure path: asserts exit 3 when SFTP is attempted and fails (with output CSVs still present on disk), exit 0 on success or when --sftp is absent, and exit 0 on --dry-run --sftp (no upload attempted).
  • tests/test_registry.py — Transformer registry: known entity lookup, DefaultTransformer fallback for unregistered entities.
  • tests/test_source_config.py — Source-config normalisation (normalize_source_config) and get_source_file() retrieval from context.
  • tests/test_helpers.py — src/utils/helpers.py utilities: normalize_columns(), ensure_directory(), district_slug(), build_zip_name(), etc.
  • tests/test_paths.py — src/utils/paths.py path helpers under both source-install and frozen-bundle (sys.frozen) scenarios.
  • tests/test_benchmarks.py — Performance benchmarks on a synthetic 5 000-student dataset (deselected from normal run; invoke with -m benchmark).
  • tests/test_property_based.py — Hypothesis property-based tests: invariants on grade mapping, email generation, and other pure functions to catch edge cases hand-written tests miss.
  • tests/test_ui_smoke.py — Playwright headless Chrome smoke tests: each Streamlit page loads without crashing and renders key structural elements; requires the streamlit_server fixture.

docs.title: docs/
docs.text:
• docs/index.md — MkDocs home page: hero section with SpacesEDU branding, product summary, and quick-links to partner/developer guides.
  • docs/partner/installation.md — Partner installation guide: prerequisites, download, Setup Wizard walkthrough (~15–20 min), Windows/Linux task-scheduler setup.
  • docs/partner/faq.md — Frequently asked questions: run frequency, supported districts, file naming, SFTP behaviour, data privacy.
  • docs/partner/troubleshooting.md — Troubleshooting guide: no-output causes, encoding errors, SFTP failures, schedule not firing, log file locations.
  • docs/partner/how-classes-work.md — Explains the three class types (homeroom, subject, blended) and how each is detected from GDE data.
  • docs/partner/headless-sftp-setup.md — Headless / Docker SFTP setup: configuring SFTP credentials entirely from the CLI (--sftp-configure, --sftp-test, --sftp-show) without a browser.
  • docs/developer/architecture.md — Architecture overview: ETL pipeline diagram, extractor/transformer/loader responsibilities, config-driven design, blended class logic.
  • docs/developer/setup.md — Developer setup: Python version, clone, pip install, running tests, linting, type checking, Streamlit UI, PyInstaller build.
  • docs/developer/testing.md — Testing guide: test categories (unit, e2e, snapshot, property-based, benchmark, UI smoke), coverage requirements, mocking patterns.
  • docs/developer/release.md — Release process: version bump, tag push, GitHub Actions automated build (3 platform binaries), GitHub Release creation, MkDocs deploy.
  • docs/developer/adding-district.md — Step-by-step guide for adding a new district YAML config with _base inheritance and non-standard file names/column mappings.
  • docs/developer/adding-transformer.md — Guide for adding a custom entity transformer class and registering it in the registry.

docs.title: Decisions (claugentic harness)
docs.text:
Append the newest decision at the top. Consult this log before re-litigating a past choice. Each entry is a dated one-liner: what was decided and why.

  • 2026-06-25 — Plan 0011: graceful UI shutdown — idle watchdog + Exit controls + single-instance guard, gated by a write_guard over the atomic loader (INVARIANTS candidate). The frozen UI exe ran sys.exit(stcli.main()) → the Streamlit server + console leaked forever after the browser tab closed (no built-in "exit on last disconnect"), and the leaked port-8501 occupant blocked relaunch. New src/ui/lifecycle.py concentrates the fix: a daemon idle watchdog (started once per process via @st.cache_resource in Home.py) reads the live browser-session count and os._exit(0)s after a 90s disconnect grace (keys off websocket-gone, not inactivity — an open-idle tab stays alive); explicit Exit DistrictSync (sidebar, shared brand.sidebar_exit_control()) + wizard Finish & Close buttons call request_exit(); and launcher.already_running(8501) opens a browser to an existing instance and exits instead of starting a second server. Three load-bearing dependencies recorded (re-verify on any streamlit bump): (1) the watchdog reaches the PRIVATE Runtime._session_mgr.num_active_sessions() — verified on streamlit 1.54.0, isolated to ONE helper (lifecycle._active_session_count) so a rename fails loudly in one place, wrapped in try/except that degrades to a logged no-op (never crashes the UI), and pinned streamlit>=1.54,<1.55 in requirements.txt + pyproject.toml [ui]/[dev] (requirements-dev.txt inherits via -r); (2) the single-instance guard keys off the /_stcore/health body == ok (not bare connectivity/200 — a non-Streamlit 8501 occupant won't match); (3) write_guard()/safe_to_exit() is the write-integrity gate — 02_Convert.py wraps its SFTP upload path (DataLoader.save_all into a TemporaryDirectory + upload_csvs) in write_guard(), and BOTH the watchdog (defers to next tick) AND request_exit() (bounded ~30s wait) refuse to exit while a write is in flight, so an exit can't truncate the in-flight SFTP upload into a partial remote delivery. Corrected at architect-review: the UI's save_all stages into a TEMP dir, not the real output dir, so it cannot tear data/output — the guard's real value is the SFTP egress, plus defense-in-depth for any future direct-to-output UI write (whose finally-based atomicity a hard os._exit would skip). should_exit (pure), write_guard/safe_to_exit, and already_running are unit-tested (tests/test_ui_lifecycle.py) despite the src/ui coverage omit; a ui-marked read-only smoke (no os._exit) asserts the private reach stays live. webbrowser.open opens a NEW tab to the existing instance (can't focus the existing tab) — accepted. launcher.already_running relies on streamlit's transitive requests dependency (bundled via --collect-all streamlit) — re-verify on a streamlit bump. CLI/scheduled-task path untouched; SD74 snapshot byte-identical (UI is outside the ETL path).
  • 2026-06-25 — Exe --version is stamped from the git tag at build time (minimal fix). Two layered defects: (1) pyproject.toml's hand-typed version drifted — v3.3.0 left it at 3.2.0 (manual bump forgotten); (2) more importantly, the distributed exe never reported a real version at all — src/main.py reads importlib.metadata.version("districtsync"), but the package is never installed and the build never --copy-metadatas it, so the frozen exe falls back to "dev" (confirmed: python -m src.main --version → DistrictSync dev; pkg not installed → PackageNotFoundError). So a pyproject-only sync would NOT have fixed the user-visible symptom. Fix (deliberately minimal — setuptools-scm considered and rejected as overkill; see superseded plan 0010): each release build job writes src/_version.py from the pushed tag (echo "version = '${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}'" > src/_version.py; gitignored; bundled via the existing --collect-submodules src + an explicit --hidden-import=src._version), and a module-level _resolve_version() in main.py reads src._version → package metadata → "dev". The git tag is the single source for the released exe's version; no manual pyproject bump is load-bearing anymore. The earlier CI guard (scripts/check_release_version.py + 14 tests, the release.yml test-job step, the release.md edits) was reverted — redundant once the version is tag-derived, and it never fixed the "dev" bug. make build-win / unbuilt source still report dev (acceptable — official builds are CI-only). No new build dependency. ETL untouched → SD74 byte-identical. Caveat: the in-bundle read is verified by-construction + locally; confirm on the first real v* exe (DistrictSync --version shows the tag, not dev).
  • 2026-06-25 — Plan 0009 Slice 2: scheduler errors de-CLIXML'd + the wizard classifies registration failures by elevation. A real failed Register-ScheduledTask surfaced PowerShell 5.1's CLIXML error serialization — a #< CLIXML … <Objs>…<S S="Error"> : Access is denied.</S></Objs> blob that echoes the whole failing script (incl. the $env:DSYNC_TASK_PW literal) and buries the human message — because Write-Error to a redirected stderr is CLIXML-wrapped. Fix at the source: the script's catch now emits the bare message via [Console]::Error.WriteLine($_.Exception.Message); exit 1 (plain text, no CLIXML; success path Write-Output 'DSYNC_OK' unchanged). Defensive Python fallback: _clean_ps_stderr(text) runs on captured stderr BEFORE the canonical-marker match — if #< CLIXML is present it extracts ONLY the first <S S="Error"> node (the message line; later nodes echo the script body, never returned), decodes _x000D_/_x000A_, strips the leading "<cmd> : " positional prefix; otherwise returns the text stripped. Bounded regex over <S S="Error">(.*?)</S> (no XML parser on untrusted text); a leak test asserts the cleaned message contains "Access is denied" but neither CLIXML/<Objs/the script body nor DSYNC_TASK_PW/the secret. So the canonical markers ("PowerShell not found" / "ScheduledTasks module not available" / Windows' "Access is denied") still match the cleaned text and register_task returns a clean one-liner. Wizard: new is_elevated() in windows.py (win32 ctypes.windll.shell32.IsUserAnAdmin() wrapped try/except → False; non-win32 → False; module-level + mypy-clean since UI is mypy-excluded). The wizard's blanket "Run as administrator" failure branch is replaced by a pure, unit-testable _classify_schedule_error(msg, elevated): not-elevated + access-denied → run-as-admin; elevated + access-denied → batch-logon-right / wrong-password (account password not Windows Hello PIN / MSA microsoft.com password) hint; "PowerShell not found" / "ScheduledTasks module not available" → their own actionable lines; else → the clean msg. Kills the misleading run-as-admin loop the already-elevated SD54 admin hit. Slice-1's leak tests still pass against the new catch. UX-only — no SD54 dependency; SD74 snapshot byte-identical (scheduler outside the ETL path).
  • 2026-06-25 — Plan 0009 Slice 1: Windows scheduled-task registration moved from schtasks /Create /XML to PowerShell Register-ScheduledTask. The 2026-06-15 long-path fix paired a /XML-declared <LogonType>Password</LogonType> with schtasks /RU /RP credentials; that handoff fails ERROR: Access is denied even when elevated (the SD54 rollout blocker). Fix: register_task now runs a FIXED PowerShell script (_build_register_script(has_password), references only $env:DSYNC_*) by base64-encoding it as UTF-16LE and passing it via powershell.exe -EncodedCommand (no script file on disk) and passes all dynamic values — incl. the password — via a fresh-copy child env (_build_env(), {**os.environ, …}; os.environ never mutated). -EncodedCommand, not stdin (-Command -): piping a multi-line try/catch to -Command - over stdin silently no-ops on PowerShell 5.1 (read line-by-line, the block returns exit 0 with no output and registers nothing → reports success while doing nothing); -EncodedCommand parses the script as one complete unit so the fail-loud try/catch actually runs. Do not revert to stdin. The unattended path forces the stored-credential logon with an explicit New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal -LogonType Password -RunLevel Highest (not the -User/-Password/-RunLevel parameter-set inference, which can silently degrade to S4U/Interactive on some PS 5.1 builds) → New-ScheduledTask -Principal → Register-ScheduledTask -InputObject $task -User -Password -Force. Password-via-env, not argv is a hardening win over the legacy /RP <pw> (which was visible to all users in the process list); the script never echoes $env:DSYNC_TASK_PW, and a leak-closure test asserts the value + the DSYNC_TASK_PW literal appear in neither the returned message nor caplog (success and failure). Settings parity with the old XML preserved (-StartWhenAvailable:$false no-catch-up, battery flags, PT2H, IgnoreNew); run time parsed with InvariantCulture (locale-safe). No-password path → -LogonType Interactive -RunLevel Limited (matches the prior InteractiveToken, never S4U; run_highest stays ignored). Fail-loud canonical messages — "PowerShell not found" (FileNotFoundError) / "ScheduledTasks module not available" (cmdlet-not-found) / else raw PS exception text verbatim — are the contract the Slice-2 wizard classifier keys off. delete_task/query_task deliberately stay on schtasks.exe (read-only/name-only, no length/credential surface, fully tested, and query_task's LIST-parse output is consumed by Home.py) — consistency migration is a tracked ROADMAP follow-up, not this slice. No new PyInstaller hidden imports (plain subprocess to a system binary). Implementer verified the no-password path live (registers Interactive, queried, deleted); the credentialed/unattended path is gated on the user live-verification checklist (LogonType=Password/RunLevel=Highest + logged-off SFTP-reachable run + Last Run Result=0x0). SD74 snapshot byte-identical (scheduler is outside the ETL path).
  • 2026-06-23 — Plan 0008 Item 1: run_transform skips an entity only when ALL its source frames are empty (was: positional-first frame empty). The old skip (primary_df = raw_data.get(source_files[0]); if primary_df.empty: continue) dropped any entity whose listed-first source was empty — wrong for an entity that resolves its bands by role (StudentAttendanceTransformer, daily/period order-independent). A period-only sd51attendance district lists daily_absences first, so an empty daily frame skipped a fully-populated period band → no StudentAttendance.csv, run exits 0 (silent). New: source_frames = [raw_data.get(sf, …) for sf in source_files]; if all(df.empty …): skip; the positional-first is still passed as the primary (may be empty for a role-resolved entity — transformers tolerate it: Enrollments/Classes return empty on empty schedule, Attendance unions only role-resolved bands). The transformed.empty net remains the final backstop, and the 0006 no-usable-input guard (keys off INPUT presence) is independent and unweakened. Reproduce-first: a period-only run_transform asserted StudentAttendance ABSENT first, now produced. SD74 byte-identical (none of its entities are role-resolved).
  • 2026-06-23 — Plan 0008 Item 2: append_year_to_id stays column-level resilient (won't-fix-by-decision). Considered making the append_year_to_id branch of apply_field_map per-row resilient (mirroring the transform: path). Declined: its helper generate_class_id performs no fallible operation (a row.get + an f-string — it cannot raise on a single row), so a per-row try/except would defend a failure that cannot occur and add a second near-duplicate resilience loop (DRY/YAGNI). The branch already blanks the whole column loudly (recorded as a column-level data_error, never silent) on any structural failure. Tightened the docstring to record this; promote to per-row only if generate_class_id ever gains a parse/IO step. No code change.
  • 2026-06-24 — Plan 0008 Item 3 (revised, post-approval): stale entity CSVs are ARCHIVED after each run, not deleted. Added DataLoader.archive_stale_outputs(emitted) -> sorted[str]: detects stale entity CSVs via the pure detect_stale_outputs(emitted) (lists <EntityName>.csv whose stem is a registry-known entity and not emitted this run), then moves each into a lazily-created output_dir/archive_<ts>/ via os.replace (atomic same-fs); run_pipeline calls it after a successful save_all and logs an INFO with the archived names (no exit/run-log change). Non-destructive os.replace move sidesteps the cross-config data-loss that blocked an auto-delete: under _base inheritance no delete allow-list is cleanly safe — a registry/mappings.keys() set would erase a DIFFERENT config's inherited-but-disabled entity CSV sharing the dir (e.g. an mbp_core-written CourseInfo.csv), and an enabled_entities set would BOTH miss a config-dropped entity AND wipe an enabled entity's last-good CSV on an empty-input run — so moving aside (recoverable bytes) is the safe primitive. Enabler: the SFTP uploader globs output_dir.glob("*.csv") — top-level only, non-recursive (uploader.py:182) — so a file inside the archive_<ts>/ SUBfolder is auto-excluded from delivery (a stale CSV can no longer ship). Best-effort: a per-file move failure is logged at ERROR and skipped (the roster already committed + delivered — an archive hiccup must not fail the run). Supersedes the earlier warn-only landing. Foot-gun (Stage-9): any future output-dir delete/prune must still derive from the resolved enabled_entities, never mappings.keys() — _base inheritance defines more entities than a config enables, so mappings.keys() includes inherited-but-disabled entities and would cause cross-config data loss.
  • 2026-06-23 — Plan 0007: save_all commit is now true all-or-none (os.replace + per-file .bak_<ts>/ backup/restore). Replaced the per-file shutil.move commit loop — non-atomic across files (a move failing on file K left output a torn mix of new 1..K-1 + stale K..N, then the staging dir was deleted so the tear was permanent) and (Windows overwrite) within a file (shutil.move's os.rename raises FileExistsError on an existing dest → copy2+unlink fallback can leave a half-written dest). New _commit_staged(sorted(staged_files), backup_dir): per file, move any existing target aside into .bak_<ts>/ then promote the staged file with os.replace (atomic same-fs overwrite); on any failure roll back in reverse applied order (remove new dest / restore backup) and re-raise the original — rollback completes before save_all's finally rmtrees .bak_<ts>/ (restore-before-cleanup invariant). New-entity path takes no backup and rolls back via unlink(missing_ok=True), never os.replace(None, …). Reproduce-first: 4 role-based os.replace-injection tests captured the torn output as a failure first. SD74 byte-identical. (Chose Option A over keep-shutil.move+backup, which leaves the within-file tear unfixed.)
  • 2026-06-23 — Plan 0006: the "fail loud, don't fake success" trio (one slice). Three "false green" defects in an unattended PII tool fixed together. (1) apply_field_map is now row-resilient + loud. Previously series.apply(func) aborted the WHOLE column if any one row raised, and the outer except Exception silently blanked it to pd.NA while the run reported success — and (corrected fact, verified at runtime) the unknown-transform raise ValueError was ALSO swallowed by that same handler (returned [<NA>, <NA>], only logged a traceback), so it never actually failed loud. New: transforms apply per-row — a row whose func raises gets pd.NA in that cell only while valid rows keep their correct value (so the accurate transform path is untouched → SD74 byte-identical); the unknown-transform / any column-level error blanks that column and continues (do NOT raise — config fail-fast validation of ALLOWED_TRANSFORMS is the cleaner fix, backlog T2.4). Every failure is recorded to the new TransformContext.data_errors ledger, logged at ERROR, and surfaced in the run-log as a data_errors summary ({total, by_field}) + Run History "Completed with N data errors". Data errors are a SEPARATE axis — ETL status stays success and the run still delivers (never silently swallowed, never fails the run for one bad row). TransformOutputs gains a third field data_errors; threaded out of run_transform via DataTransformer.data_errors. (2) No-usable-input guard in run_pipeline right after extractor.load_data: not raw_data or all(df.empty …) → ERROR naming the missing/empty files + raise → existing outer handler writes _emit_run_log("failed") + re-raises → main exit 1. Keys off INPUT presence (raw_data), not outputs=={} — chosen so a supported period-only sd51attendance run (period file present, daily absent) does NOT false-fail: outputs=={} would have, because run_transform's positional-primary skip drops StudentAttendance when the daily file (its listed-first source) is empty (a separate latent bug this guard neither masks nor fixes). A partial run with some empty sources stays exit 0 by design. (3) Run History honest Status (display-only, additive): the at-a-glance Status cell shows amber "ETL OK · SFTP FAILED" / "Completed with N data errors" derived from the same booleans already in each record (no exit-code or run-log status change; the dedicated SFTP ❌ column is unchanged). SD74 snapshot byte-identical (a clean run fires none of the new branches).
  • 2026-06-23 — Plan 0005 Slice 3: anomaly compute single-sourced; diff stays per-surface. Factored the pure row-count-drop computation into pipeline.compute_anomalies(outputs, output_dir) -> list[str] (one ANOMALY_THRESHOLD = 0.20, plain "{entity} dropped from {prev} to {new} rows ({pct}% decrease)" base messages, no logging/printing). The CLI's _check_anomalies is now a thin wrapper that adds its existing presentation (the ANOMALY: prefix + logger.warning, preserving today's log output exactly); the Convert page deletes its duplicated _check_anomalies_ui + the second ANOMALY_THRESHOLD literal and calls the shared compute_anomalies, rendering each base message as st.warning(f"Anomaly detected: {msg}") + the existing caption. _compute_diff left untouched — diff stays per-surface (its documented pyarrow/Arrow string-coercion workaround is irreducibly UI-specific). Single source of truth for the anomaly business logic + threshold; both surfaces keep their user-visible wording. New TestComputeAnomalies unit tests assert the shared list both surfaces consume. SD74 snapshot unaffected.
  • 2026-06-23 — Plan 0005 Slice 2b: the Convert page is now a thin adapter over the shared engine; CLI↔UI byte-parity locked. 02_Convert.py run_conversion deletes its inline transform loop / school-year block / ordered + extra field-ordering and delegates to run_transform (returns TransformOutputs); both write paths route through DataLoader — SFTP via save_all, and the download zip + per-file buttons via the new DataLoader.csv_encoding(entity) helper (the single source of truth for the BOM rule, extracted from _write_csv so the download path stops hardcoding utf-8-sig). This is the direct fix for the audit Tier-1 "Browser Convert writes StudentAttendance.csv with a BOM." Two deliberate, desirable behavior changes (now explicit + tested): (a) a missing field-map column makes the UI write fail loud (DataLoader._write_csv raises ValueError, surfaced as st.error) instead of writing a partial file; (b) the UI writes exactly the contract columns (df[field_order], extras dropped). A fourth honest behavior change: the old Convert page caught load errors per file and continued with the remaining uploads, whereas the new adapter wraps the whole upload batch in one load_from_bytes try — so a single unparseable upload now aborts the entire conversion (desirable: matches the CLI's load_data abort-on-ExtractionError; no partial/divergent run from a bad file). New tests/test_pipeline_parity.py runs the same synthetic GDE bytes (sd51myedbc — 5 rostering + StudentAttendance) through both run_pipeline and the UI adapter path and asserts per-entity frame-equality AND on-disk read_bytes()-equality, explicitly covering StudentAttendance (no BOM) + Students (BOM) and the identical CLI/UI column sets. SD74 snapshot byte-identical (unaffected).
  • 2026-06-22 — Attendance Absence Date format is now per-district configurable (global_config.attendance.date_format, default yyyy-MM-dd). A district may override the StudentAttendance output date shape in its mapping; SD51 uses the default ISO. Uses friendly tokens (yyyy yy MMMM MMM MM dd + literal separators — not raw strftime codes) so non-technical config authors read/write it the way they speak (yyyy-MM-dd, dd-MMM-yyyy). BaseTransformer.friendly_date_format_to_strftime() translates and validates fail-loud: an unrecognized alphabetic token (e.g. a lowercase mm typo) raises at the boundary instead of silently emitting a constant/garbled date. Zero-padded tokens only (Windows strftime lacks portable non-padded directives). Resolved + validated ONCE per run in StudentAttendanceTransformer.transform() and reused by both bands. New BaseTransformer.format_date(value, strftime_fmt) shares the flexible input-parse grid with normalize_iso_date (factored into _coerce_date), so input-format recognition is unchanged. No Pydantic change (attendance is an open dict, threaded through to_raw_dict). The base StudentAttendance.field_map "Absence Date" placeholder was reduced to a direct mapping (its value was never executed; the date format's single source of truth is now attendance.date_format). SD74 snapshot byte-identical (attendance not enabled there).
  • 2026-06-22 — Attendance Absence Date now emitted as ISO yyyy-MM-dd (was DD-MMM-YYYY). SpacesEDU's standalone attendance importer rejects non-ISO date shapes — the "Invalid date format" error that the 2026-06-19 BOM decision attributed to the UTF-8 BOM was only partly the BOM; the DD-MMM-YYYY shape itself is also rejected. StudentAttendanceTransformer now formats both bands' dates with the existing normalize_iso_date (input GDE dates in any of the 4 recognized formats — dd-MMM-yyyy / ISO / m/d/yyyy / d/m/yyyy — still normalize to ISO, so input-format intelligence is unchanged). Reverses only the OUTPUT shape; rostering dates are untouched (only the standalone attendance feed was failing). Removed the now-dead format_dd_mmm_yyyy helper — a near-clone of normalize_iso_date differing only in its strftime (DRY / no dead code) — and swapped it for normalize_iso_date in ALLOWED_TRANSFORMS and the base StudentAttendance.field_map transform placeholder (that field_map's values are decorative: the transformer builds rows directly and never calls apply_field_map). ISO is a fixed SpacesEDU contract (not per-district), so it's hardcoded, not configurable (KISS/YAGNI). SD74 snapshot byte-identical (attendance not enabled there).
  • 2026-06-19 — Plan 0004: StudentAttendance.csv written WITHOUT the UTF-8 BOM. SpacesEDU's standalone attendance import rejected the file — "Unexpected file: StudentAttendance.csv" + cascading "Invalid date format" — even though the data matched the spec (valid DD-MMM-YYYY dates, 4 case-sensitive columns). Root cause: DataLoader._write_csv wrote every CSV with utf-8-sig (BOM), and SpacesEDU's BOM-strict, case-sensitive header check read the first header as ﻿School Number ≠ School Number, so the file wasn't recognized and column mapping broke (mis-validating the date column). Fix: a DataLoader._NO_BOM_ENTITIES = {"StudentAttendance"} allowlist — those entities are written plain UTF-8 (no BOM); all other CSVs keep the utf-8-sig BOM so districts can still open them in Excel without mojibake. The rostering CSVs were unaffected because they reach SpacesEDU via the zip/OneRoster path, which tolerates the BOM; only the standalone attendance feed is strict. Regression test asserts StudentAttendance has no BOM and a rostering entity still does. (CRLF line endings kept — RFC-4180 standard; revisit only if SpacesEDU rejects them next.)
  • 2026-06-19 — Plan 0004: StudentAttendance output reduced from 28 columns to the 4 required SpacesEDU fields. The SpacesEDU docx explicitly permits dropping every optional field after Student Number ("Only required fields are needed in the file, all other fields can be removed if preferred"), so StudentAttendance.csv now emits ONLY the 4 populated columns in exact case-sensitive order: School Number, Absence Date, Absence Category, Student Number. Dropped the 24 always-blank optional columns. OUTPUT_COLUMNS/POPULATED_COLUMNS consolidated to one constant; _frame() no longer reindexes to a 28-col superset with fill_value="" (rows already carry exactly the 4 keys). The base myedbc StudentAttendance.field_map was reduced to the 4 keys (field_map is used ONLY for output column ORDER here — the transformer builds rows directly and never calls apply_field_map, so the values stay placeholders). All band logic preserved (role-based resolution, fail-loud derivation, no drop_duplicates, blank-code/blank-student drops, format_dd_mmm_yyyy). SD51 row count unchanged (79,303); SD74 snapshot byte-identical (not enabled).
  • 2026-06-19 — Plan 0004: StudentAttendance band selection moved to per-district source_files (role-based, order-independent). The base myedbc entity previously forced BOTH bands by declaring daily_absences + period_absences in StudentAttendance.source_files; deep-merge can't remove an inherited source_files key, so a single-band district couldn't run only daily or only period. Fix: the base now declares no source_files for StudentAttendance (empty {}; it never enables the entity, so it never runs there) and keeps only the shared defaults all districts inherit — the two headerless headers: blocks, the 28-col field_map, and global_config.attendance with both daily and period sub-blocks. Each district declares the role(s) for the band(s) it runs (daily_absences K-7 and/or period_absences 8-12); SD51 declares both (daily first → stays the pipeline primary). The transformer now resolves each band by its ROLE from context.raw_data (via get_source_file) instead of treating the positional primary df as the daily frame — so a period-only district (whose period file is primary) is no longer mis-processed as K-7 daily. Each band is optional/graceful and each band's config block is required only when that band's data is present (attendance.daily gated on the daily frame, attendance.period on the period frame), so a single-band district need not configure the other. SD51 output byte-identical (it declares both, daily first → get_source_file(daily_absences) returns the same frame the pipeline passed as df); SD74 snapshot byte-identical (not enabled). sd51attendance (attendance-only tier, _base: sd51myedbc) inherits both bands. No Pydantic change (EntityConfig.source_files already accepts an empty dict).
  • 2026-06-17 — claugentic-dev-harness installed alongside the project's pre-existing in-house harness. DistrictSync already maintains its own docs/WORKFLOW.md, docs/ENGINEERING_STANDARDS.md, docs/DECISIONS.md, docs/ROADMAP.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE_TREE.md, .claude/agents/ roles, and a scripts/check_architecture_tree.py gate. The claugentic harness installs under the claugentic- namespace (its own tree + a second tree-gate hook, per the adopter's init choice) so the two coexist without collision. The harness composes with the project's existing CI gates (ruff / mypy / bandit / pytest 80% / SD74 snapshot / config-validation), it does not replace them. (init harvest of docs/WORKFLOW.md.)

  • 2026-06-17 — Load-bearing project invariants the harness must respect (harvested from docs/WORKFLOW.md + docs/DECISIONS.md, recorded so claugentic agents don't violate them): (1) Config-driven columns — GDE source column names come from the district field_map, never hardcoded in transformers; (2) Zero-orphan invariant — enrollment/class rows are filtered to context.active_student_ids so nothing references a student absent from Students.csv; (3) _base config inheritance — all 7 entity definitions live in base myedbc_mapping.yaml; configs select via enabled_entities, never redefine entities. See the project's own docs/DECISIONS.md for full detail.

  • 2026-06-19 — Plan 0004 Slice 3: StudentAttendance 8-12 Student Period Absences band (per-period pass-through). Added the 8-12 band as per-period PASS-THROUGH unioned with the existing K-7 daily band into the single 28-column StudentAttendance.csv. One output row per period-absence row — no AM/PM collapse, no Period Id, no derivation — because SpacesEDU aggregates per-day itself via per-entry weights (8-12 absence = 0.25/entry → 4 entries = 1 day, capped at 1/day); the standard rotating-block timetable export has no AM/PM signal anyway, so collapsing would be guesswork. Category passed through AS-IS (the GDE Absence Category already holds the final codes A/A-E/L/AD/AL/OffSite/ISS…; SpacesEDU ignores non-accepted ones, so no filtering). Rows with a blank category OR blank student number are dropped; no dedup (per-period multiplicity is intentional). Source = the standard headerless StudentPeriodAbsences.txt (MyEd Data Elements §2.45, 17 cols) — NOT the Enhanced variant the original plan assumed (Enhanced is headed + adds a Period Id the pass-through model doesn't need; standard is consistent with the headerless StudentDailyAbsences.txt). daily_absences stays FIRST in source_files so it remains the pipeline's primary (skip-on-empty-primary guard). New global_config.attendance.period block (4 source column names, no derivation knobs); reuses the open-dict attendance model from Slice 2 (no Pydantic change). Both bands optional/graceful (daily empty → period only; period absent → daily only). SD51 inherits the base source filenames unchanged (no per-district override). SD74 snapshot byte-identical; K-7 daily behavior unchanged. This supersedes the original Slice-3 plan (Enhanced-file half-day collapse), which was parked pending an Enhanced re-export.

  • 2026-06-18 — Plan 0004 Slice 2: StudentAttendance entity scaffold + K-7 Daily band (SD51). New opt-in StudentAttendanceTransformer emits the 28-column SpacesEDU StudentAttendance.csv (4 columns populated, 24 blank, no drop_duplicates — a full-day absence is intentionally two identical rows). Derivation is fully config-resident (Configurable-Columns + fail-loud): the K-7 (Absent Code, Authorized) → Absence Category map, the Portion Absent → row-count rule, and every source column name live in a new global_config.attendance.daily block read at runtime — so SpacesEDU's review tunes config, never code. An unmapped non-blank (code, authorized) pair raises rather than silently dropping/mis-bucketing. attendance was added to the GlobalConfig Pydantic model as an open dict (not a nested model) and threaded through to_raw_dict() so it reaches the transformer and so Slice 3's 8-12 Enhanced-Period keys need no model change. New format_dd_mmm_yyyy transform (added to ALLOWED_TRANSFORMS) produces the contract's DD-MMM-YYYY shape (mirrors normalize_iso_date). SD51 enables it via a full enabled_entities list (base-5 rostering + StudentAttendance) because deep-merge replaces lists. Quality report gets an explicit StudentAttendance: [] skip (duplicates legitimate). SD74 snapshot byte-identical (not enabled for SD74); no other district affected. The 8-12 Enhanced Period Absences band is deferred to Slice 3 (pending SD51's StudentPeriodAbsencesEnhanced.txt re-export).

docs.title: Project history (harvested from the in-house harness, 2026-06-17)
2026-06-15 — Windows scheduled-task registration via Task Scheduler XML
docs.text:
SUPERSEDED-BY-0009 (2026-06-25). The schtasks /Create /XML + <LogonType>Password</LogonType> + /RU /RP credential handoff below regressed: it fails ERROR: Access is denied on the unattended (password) path even when elevated — the very "Verified LIVE … no iteration needed" claim that broke. Registration is now PowerShell Register-ScheduledTask (see the 2026-06-25 entry at the top). The /TR 261-char insight and the no-catch-up StartWhenAvailable=false rationale below still hold and are preserved in the new mechanism; the schtasks /XML + /RP mechanism is dead. delete_task/query_task remain on schtasks.exe. Retained for history.

  • Register via schtasks /Create /XML, not inline /TR. schtasks caps the /TR value at 261 chars; the source-mode command (cmd /c "cd /d "<repo>" && "<python>" -m src.main --sis … --input "…" --output "…" --sftp") hit 458 chars on this dev box and failed. XML has no length cap, sets <WorkingDirectory> natively (kills the brittle nested-quote cmd /c "cd /d …" wrapper), and carries the schedule/principal/run-level. Public API of register_task/query_task/delete_task/current_run_as_user unchanged; validation-before-OS-call and /RP password redaction preserved.
  • Password stays out of the XML; run-level moves into it. The run-as password is still passed via schtasks /RP on the command line (never written to the temp XML); /RU /RP /XML is a valid documented combination. The old /RL HIGHEST flag is replaced by <RunLevel>HighestAvailable</RunLevel> (password mode) / LeastPrivilege (no-password → <LogonType>InteractiveToken</LogonType>, matching the prior logged-on-only default).
  • XML written as UTF-16-with-BOM (encoding="utf-16" + <?xml … encoding="UTF-16"?>) — schtasks /Create /XML accepted it first try on Windows 11 (no iteration needed); UTF-16 chosen for cross-version compatibility. Temp file is always removed in a finally.
  • Hand-built XML string with xml.sax.saxutils.escape on every interpolated value (paths/user/args) rather than ElementTree — deterministic, testable output with explicit escaping at the boundary (validate-at-boundary). Daily trigger uses a fixed past StartBoundary date (2024-01-01) so the emitted XML is deterministic and never triggers a catch-up run (<StartWhenAvailable>false</StartWhenAvailable>).
  • Verified LIVE on this Windows 11 box end-to-end (register→query→delete) with a deliberately long input/output path: old /TR would have been 458 chars (>261) and failed; XML registered SUCCESS, queried Ready with the full command line intact, deleted cleanly.

docs.title: 2026-06-19 — PreReg included by default (reverts v3.2.0 PreReg-excluded default)
docs.text:
• PreReg included by default. Default active_values = ["Active", "PreReg"], matching the SpacesEDU / myBlueprint+ Advanced CSV spec's expected EnrollStatus values (exactly Active and PreReg). The v3.2.0 default (["Active"] only — see the superseded 2026-06-05 entry below) was a breaking change that silently dropped pre-registered students from Students/Classes/Enrollments. Reverted because the spec lists PreReg as an expected, includable status.
  • Fix lives in code, not per-district mapping. Change is to BaseTransformer.DEFAULT_ACTIVE_VALUES (src/etl/transformers/base.py), so every config gets the corrected default without editing each config/mappings/*.yaml.
  • Districts can still opt out or extend. A district can EXCLUDE PreReg again, or ADD statuses (e.g. "Active No Primary"), via EnrollStatus.active_values in its config — the per-config override is the single point of customization.
  • Withdraw-date logic unchanged. Status still wins when present (active iff in active_values; withdraw date ignored); only rows with no status value fall back to the withdraw date (past/unparseable → Inactive). "Active No Primary" and other non-listed statuses remain Inactive by default.

docs.title: 2026-06-05 — Plan 0003 verification + import model (full-snapshot, upsert)
docs.text:
• Verified end-to-end on real SD48 GDE data (after rebasing onto the post-mbponly/extractor main): roster 7,922 → 7,543 (−379 = 50 inactive-without-withdraw-date leak + 329 PreReg, all verifiably non-active); orphaned student enrollments 405 → 0 on a full --sis sd48myedbc run (not just fixtures), stable under the rewritten extractor. SD74 snapshot byte-identical.
  • GDEs are full snapshots, not deltas. Of 6,278 students in the SD48 schedule, all but 1 are present in the demographic extract — so the orphans were withdrawn/inactive students in the extract (removed by the active rule), not active students missing from a delta. The 1 exception (2644905 — in the schedule, absent from the demographic) is a source-data gap → ROADMAP.
  • SpacesEDU import is upsert/unenroll-style (docs/partner/faq.md): it preserves existing classes, adds new enrollments, and unenrolls students who drop out of Enrollments.csv. So dropping a withdrawn student's enrollment cleanly unenrolls them — which is why the zero-orphan filter is correct (the intended outcome), not merely "avoids import errors".

docs.title: 2026-06-05 — Zero-orphan enrollments (plan 0003, Slice 2)
docs.text:
• Zero-orphan invariant via a published active roster. StudentTransformer publishes its filtered output's User ID set to context.active_student_ids (so active_student_ids == set(Students.User ID) by construction); classes.py (homeroom) and enrollments.py (homeroom + subject) then filter their student rows to that set. Result: no enrollment or homeroom class references a student absent from Students.csv. Verified on an SD48-shaped fixture: 3 orphans (2 homeroom + 1 subject) → 0. The single filter helper is BaseTransformer.filter_to_active(df, student_col, context, caller) — both consumers call it (DRY); chosen over re-deriving "is active" at each read site (would drift from the roster). Demographic Student Number and schedule Student ID share the pupil-number value space; both sides normalized with astype(str).str.strip().
  • Students-only filter; teacher/co-teacher rows untouched. Only the student-row derivations are filtered; teacher rows in _homeroom_enrollments/_subject_enrollments derive from the unfiltered frames, and _blended_teacher_enrollments/_classinfo_coteacher_enrollments are not touched — so teacher rows are byte-identical to before (regression-tested by holding the homeroom class set constant and comparing a mixed roster vs an all-active control). The one intended teacher-row change: a homeroom with only inactive students is dropped (no empty homeroom class) along with its teacher enrollment — the no-empty-homeroom goal, not a leak.
  • Never filter-to-empty (guard). When active_student_ids is empty (Students disabled / ran later, e.g. mbp_core) or the student column is absent, filter_to_active logs a WARNING and returns the frame unchanged rather than dropping every row. filter_to_active returns a .copy() so callers can mutate columns without a SettingWithCopyWarning (matches the other filter_* helpers).
  • SD74 snapshot byte-identical. SD74 has no inactive students, so its active set = all students → the filter is a no-op; the frozen snapshot stays green (the regression guard).

docs.title: 2026-06-05 — Active-student predicate (plan 0003, Slice 1)
docs.text:
• Config-driven active predicate (single source of truth). Active-student detection now lives in BaseTransformer (resolve_active_config / compute_enroll_status / is_active_mask), resolving the status / withdraw-date columns and active_values from the Students EnrollStatus config (Configurable Columns). students.py delegates to it. Replaces the hardcoded-column logic in students.py._determine_enrollment_status. (Slice 2 will reuse the same mask to drop orphan enrollments.)
  • Corrected status-column alias. The default status column resolves from the alias ["enrollment status", "enrolment status"] — first present in the (normalized) frame. Real MyEd BC exports use the two-L "Enrollment status"; the repo fixtures / SD40's injected headers use the one-L "Enrolment Status". The old code only matched one-L, so real two-L data silently fell through to withdraw-date-only detection (the SD48 −50 leak). A district can override status_column explicitly.
  • Status wins; withdraw date is the fallback. When a row has a status value, the status decides (active iff in active_values) and the withdraw date is ignored — so a re-enrolled student (status Active with a lingering past withdraw date) stays Active. Only rows with no status value fall back to the withdraw date (past/unparseable → Inactive + one aggregated warning; 4 formats %d-%b-%Y, %Y-%m-%d, %m/%d/%Y, %d/%m/%Y). This matches the original status-XOR-date intent — corrected from a briefly-shipped "withdraw-date hard override" that wrongly dropped re-enrolled students (SD48: 3 such, roster 7,543 → 7,548).
  • PreReg excluded by default. (SUPERSEDED — see 2026-06-19.) Default active_values = ["Active"], matching docs/partner/faq.md ("Students with status PreReg … are excluded"). The active mask is label != "Inactive" (no implicit union with "Active"), so a district can opt PreReg (or any status) back in via EnrollStatus.active_values — e.g. for upcoming-year rosters. (An earlier draft of this plan retained PreReg; corrected after confirming the documented partner behavior against the SD48 data.)
  • EnrollStatus config fails loudly. New strict FieldEnrollStatus (extra="forbid") validates the dict form; a recognizable EnrollStatus override carrying an unknown/typo'd key now RAISES at config load (was a silent warn-passthrough). Bare EnrollStatus: null stays the auto-detect sentinel. SD48 roster correction: −50 verifiably-inactive students (Withdrawn/Inactive/Graduate without a withdraw date). SD74 snapshot stays byte-identical (its demographic has no status column + all-blank withdraw dates → date-only back-compat).
  • Deferred (ROADMAP): classify_field is not idempotent — re-validating an EntityConfig that already holds typed field values (e.g. nested in a freshly-constructed MappingConfig) stringifies them. Pre-existing; out of scope for this slice. The real load_config path (raw dict → validation) is unaffected.

docs.title: 2026-06-05 — Plan 0002 Slice 4 (unattended SFTP + schedule hardening)
docs.text:
• Scheduled task runs as the interactive setup user with stored Windows password (/RU /RP /RL HIGHEST) so it survives reboots; SYSTEM rejected — SYSTEM cannot read the per-user OS keyring where the SFTP password is stored.
  • SFTP secrets stay in the per-user OS keyring; the task's run-as account must equal the setup account — the Setup Wizard enforces this by resolving current_run_as_user() and registering the task as that account.
  • A requested SFTP upload that fails exits code 3 (ETL output CSVs still written), so delivery failures surface visibly in Task Scheduler's "Last Run Result" and in Run History, not silently as a green exit 0.

docs.title: 2026-06-04 — Harness / standards
docs.text:
• Output targeting via enabled_entities. All 7 entity definitions live in base myedbc_mapping.yaml; configs select which to emit via global_config.enabled_entities. Tiers mbp_all (7) and mbp_core (Students + 2 course CSVs) are thin overrides. Rationale: defines entity templates once (DRY) and lets a per-district myBlueprint+ config (_base: <district> + enabled_entities) inherit both the district's columns and the base entity defs. Chosen over "the new config carries the new sections" (would duplicate defs and hit YAML's single-_base limit). Ratified after PR #12 review.
  • ARCHITECTURE_TREE.md + presence enforcement. Added docs/ARCHITECTURE_TREE.md (one line per source file). scripts/check_architecture_tree.py is a deterministic gate (no LLM) checking presence + staleness for src/**/*.py and config/mappings/*.yaml; descriptions are authored by humans/agents. Intended wiring: make check-tree + CI (authoritative) + optional local pre-commit.
  • Configurable-columns standard reaffirmed. GDE source columns must be resolved from field_map, never hardcoded in transformer code. student_courses.py (PR #12) violates this (hardcodes ~10 columns, bypasses field_map for input) — logged as debt to migrate.
  • Line-ending hygiene (proposed). Working tree shows widespread CRLF↔LF churn; recommend a committed .gitattributes (* text=auto eol=lf) + one normalization commit to stop the noise.
  • Adopted the staged agent Development Workflow (docs/WORKFLOW.md): triage → discuss → plan (.claude/plans/) → adversarial plan-review → spec → user approval → implement (isolated) → verify → land/archive → retrospect. Roles are a library in .claude/agents/ the orchestrator selects from per task (starter: plan-reviewer, implementer-architect). Hard rule: slice work into units one specialist finishes in a single ≤1M-context session, landing complete with no new tech debt. Stage 9 feeds learnings back into the harness (living-harness principle). Requires un-ignoring .claude/agents/ + .claude/plans/ so the role library and plans are shared (see .gitignore).
  • Architecture-improvement program → ROADMAP. The full-codebase architect review (6-agent pass) findings are captured as a tiered backlog in docs/ROADMAP.md; each item runs through the Development Workflow in its own session rather than one large change.
  • Role library = local, version-controlled, shared; NO runtime injection. Agent roles live in .claude/agents/ (committed, grow over time, shared via the repo / packageable as a plugin later). We do not fetch role/skill text from online marketplaces at runtime and inject it into subagents — that's a prompt-injection / supply-chain / non-reproducibility risk. Marketplaces (incl. third-party aggregators like skills.sh / skillsmp.com) are authoring-time discovery only: review → vendor a pinned copy → commit. Prefer Anthropic first-party sources; treat aggregators as untrusted.
  • ARCHITECTURE_TREE enforced by hooks. .claude/settings.json wires scripts/check_architecture_tree.py --hook as a PostToolUse(Write) nudge (prompts the agent to document a newly-created in-scope file, with a one-line description) + a Stop backstop (blocks finishing until the index is current). Script = deterministic presence/staleness gate; description authored by the agent that made the file.

docs.title: 2026-05 — PR #12 (myBlueprint+ tier)
docs.text:
• Homeroom-enrollments fix. _homeroom_enrollments now resolves the demographic student-ID column from the Students User ID mapping, not the schedule-oriented Enrollments student_id_col. Fixes silently-dropped homeroom enrollments for configs where the schedule and demographic files name the ID column differently (myedbc/sd48/sd51). No-op for sd40/sd74 (they already override to Student Number).
  • Date of Birth → ISO. Students DOB normalized to yyyy-mm-dd (was dd-MMM-yyyy) to match Classes dates. Pass-through for already-ISO / unrecognized formats (never blanks valid data).
  • PreRegSchoolCode → "Next school code" (was "Previous school number"). ⚠️ PENDING: verify the real GDE column is named exactly Next school code, else it silently blanks for all districts. Currently untested.

docs.title: Engineering Standards
docs.text:
The engineering quality bar is a modular catalog under docs/claugentic-standards/ — scoped modules loaded only when relevant, anchored to ISO/IEC 25010:2023. This file is the thin entry point.

Start here → docs/claugentic-standards/README.md — the catalog index, the meta-rules (select-don't-skip, additive, novel-patterns-allowed), the two-tier global/local model, and versioning. The module contract is docs/claugentic-standards/_TEMPLATE.md.

How it plugs into the workflow: the spec (Stage 4) names the in-scope modules/dimensions; implementer-architect builds to them; architect-reviewer audits against them; "done" = the in-scope dimensions pass (see docs/claugentic-WORKFLOW.md → Definition of Done).

Capability modules (Redis, queues, storage, …) are authored just-in-time, when a change pulls one in.

Current scope (per repo). This file is a managed copy, so the per-repo scope does not live here. The init skill seeds it in the adopter repo's CLAUDE.md harness: section (a local, non-managed spot): a living, non-capping snapshot of which dimensions/modules are LIVE in that repo today (relevance is always a per-change judgment; it grows as the stack grows). The plugin ships only the universal catalog and does not ship a populated Current scope.

docs.title: Invariants (claugentic harness)
docs.text:
Load-bearing constraints that must stay true or something breaks. Each entry is a non-obvious "must hold" rule that already bit (or would bite) if a future change "simplified" it. Consult this before changing the named subsystem.

  • The idle-watchdog's reach into Streamlit internals is pinned + isolated — re-verify on any streamlit bump. (Plan 0011, 2026-06-25 · src/ui/lifecycle.py.) The graceful-shutdown watchdog reads the live browser-session count via the private streamlit.runtime.get_instance()._session_mgr.num_active_sessions() (verified on streamlit 1.54.0), and the single-instance guard keys off the /_stcore/health body == ok. These are unstable Streamlit internals, so the dependency is pinned streamlit>=1.54,<1.55 (requirements.txt + pyproject.toml [ui]/[dev]; requirements-dev.txt inherits via -r), the private reach is isolated to one helper (lifecycle._active_session_count), and it degrades to a logged no-op if the internal shape changes (never crashes the UI — the Exit button is the manual fallback). On any streamlit upgrade past 1.54, re-verify all three: _session_mgr.num_active_sessions(), the /_stcore/health body, and that already_running's transitive requests dep is still bundled (--collect-all streamlit).
